RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: second lieutenant
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: A freshly commissioned officer wearing a single gold bar. Their authority outweighs their experience — for now.

RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: first lieutenant
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: A junior officer trusted with leading a platoon, or filling a junior staff appointment under a senior officer's wing.

RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: captain
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: A company commander or senior staff officer. The first rank where strategy begins to outweigh tactics.

RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: major
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: A field-grade officer coordinating multiple companies, often serving as a battalion's executive or operations officer.

RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: lieutenant colonel
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: A senior field officer commanding a battalion, or serving as a regiment's second-in-command.

RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: colonel
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: The commanding officer of a regiment, or a principal staff officer at the highest formation levels. Their word moves whole formations.

RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: brigadier general
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: A one-star general commanding a brigade. The first rank to wear a star — and the first to truly answer for the lives of thousands.

RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: major general
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: A two-star general commanding a division. Their orders set the rhythm of a campaign.

RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: lieutenant general
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: A three-star general commanding a corps or a theater of operations. Wars hinge on their judgement.

RocketsRanks.RankDef.label: general
RocketsRanks.RankDef.description: A four-star general at the apex of professional command, answerable only to civilian leadership.
